title: Codonoprasum flavum
uses: Codonoprasum flavum is not recognized as a valid botanical name, but there may be confusion with Codonopsis, a plant in the family Campanulaceae. However, Codonopsis pilosula is used in traditional Chinese medicine and is believed to have various health benefits. The roots of Codonopsis pilosula are said to have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help with respiratory issues and other conditions. Some of its medicinal uses include relieving coughs, sore throats, and bronchitis. Additionally, it is believed to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects.

intro: Codonoprasum flavum, commonly known as yellow thyme, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the family Apiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as to soothe digestive problems and relieve pain. The bioactive constituents of Codonoprasum flavum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Codonoprasum flavum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to promote relaxation, improve respiratory health, and support digestive well-being.
